commit.message:
fix: replace stale build RPATHs with @loader_path on macOS and normalize tag prefix in test workflows

macOS arm64 crashes with "Abort trap: 6" because SIP strips DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH
from hardened-runtime dotnet processes, leaving stale CI runner RPATHs unresolvable.
Fix by running install_name_tool after copying binaries to set @loader_path.
Also normalize workflow_dispatch tag input to always include v prefix.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: clear stale auth tokens on login page mount

Proactively clears any Supabase localStorage keys when the login page
loads, BEFORE useAuth/getSession() runs. This prevents the infinite
spinner caused by getSession() hanging on corrupt/expired tokens stuck
in Web Locks.

The login page should always start from a clean auth state — if a user
has a valid session, getSession() resolves instantly and the redirect
fires. Only stale tokens cause the hang, and those need clearing anyway.

commit.message:
Unit 8: Per-query dynamic context selection

Instead of one shared 256-token context for all query drugs, each drug gets
its own K context tokens selected from the full pool by:
    score(q, ctx) = cosine_sim(drug_q, drug_ctx) × ppr_ctx

A kinase-inhibitor query drug now attends to context tokens where the pool
drug looks like a kinase inhibitor; an allosteric binder gets different
tokens. Fully parallel — transformer runs as [N_qry, K+1, D] batch.

Changes:
- GraphBiasedMHA: handles [B, K] per-query ppr/trust (2D) alongside [K]
  shared (1D), detected by ctx_ppr.dim()
- ProteinLigandTNP.forward_per_query: batched per-query transformer forward,
  per-query affinity anchoring (each drug anchors to its own context mean)
- TNPContextBuilder.build_per_query_context: collects full pool, subsamples
  to max_pool=4096 by PPR weight, scores by drug_sim×PPR via matmul, returns
  top-K per query as [N_qry, K, ...] tensors
- run_streaming_exp_tnp.py: --per-query-k flag (default 0 = shared context)

Usage: add --per-query-k 64 (or 128) to enable Unit 8

commit.message:
Use kill -9 to ensure Claude/Codex apps fully exit before relaunch

pkill (SIGTERM) on Electron apps can be caught or delayed — the app
restarts via macOS crash guard before the new open -b fires, inheriting
the old (stale) environment. kill -9 (SIGKILL) guarantees termination;
bump sleep to 0.5s to give the OS time to reap the process tree.

commit.message:
fix: detect zombie agents in genesis consistency check

Agents registered before the TransferFromBucket onboarding fix (595d71f)
received no allocation because the ecosystem bucket was empty at
registration time. The bucket-sum check passes (TotalSupply is still
correct), so a second check is needed.

Zombie-agent check: if the identity registry has ≥1 registered agent
but the ecosystem bucket still holds exactly genesis.EcosystemAllocation
(no onboarding transfer has ever drawn from it), the store is stale. On
AETHERNET_TESTNET=true this triggers a wipe so agents re-register with
the funded ecosystem bucket and receive their onboarding allocation.

Also updates checkGenesisConsistency signature to accept *identity.Registry.

commit.message:
refactor(deploy): single-file CF Worker + D1 with worker-logs RPC (#4)

* refactor(db): migrate from better-sqlite3 to bun:sqlite

Switch to Bun's built-in SQLite driver to eliminate native binding
compilation in Docker and simplify deployment. Adds DB_PATH env var
support for configuring database location in production.

- Replace better-sqlite3 + @types with bun:sqlite (built-in)
- Add DB_PATH env var override (defaults to project root)
- Update query type signatures to bun:sqlite generic order
- Export app from index.ts for integration tests
- Add tests/ to tsconfig include

Co-Authored-By: Arc <224894192+arc0btc@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore(deploy): add Dockerfile, fly.toml, CI, and env example

Packages the app for production deployment:
- Dockerfile using oven/bun:1, mounts /data for SQLite persistence
- .dockerignore excludes test files and local DBs
- fly.toml for Fly.io: shared-cpu-1x, 256MB, /data volume
- .env.example documents PORT and DB_PATH
- GitHub Actions CI: bun install + bun test on push/PR

Co-Authored-By: Arc <224894192+arc0btc@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(deploy): replace Docker/Fly.io with Cloudflare Worker + D1

- Remove Dockerfile, .dockerignore, fly.toml
- Add wrangler.toml with D1 database binding
- Add src/worker.ts as CF Worker entry point
- Extract DbClient interface (src/types.ts) with two implementations:
  - src/db-bun.ts for local dev (bun:sqlite)
  - src/db-d1.ts for production (Cloudflare D1)
- Refactor src/app.ts as Hono app factory (takes DbClient)
- Routes use async DB client from Hono context
- Keep bun local dev working (bun run dev)
- Add wrangler scripts (cf:dev, deploy)
- Update CLAUDE.md with new architecture

* refactor: single-file CF Worker with worker-logs RPC and production routing

- Replace multi-file architecture (app.ts, auth.ts, db-*.ts, routes/, worker.ts)
  with single src/index.ts Hono app directly on D1
- Switch wrangler.toml → wrangler.jsonc with worker-logs RPC service binding
- Production-only env (hub.aibtc.com), drop staging
- Add schema.sql for D1 initialization
- Add /llms.txt and /.well-known/agent.json discovery endpoints
- Add request logging middleware via worker-logs RPC (fire-and-forget)
- CORS enabled for public read access
- Clean package.json: hono + CF workers types only

Co-Authored-By: Arc <224894192+arc0btc@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: remove dead code, batch capability inserts, clean config

- Remove unused jsonOk helper (all routes use c.json())
- Deduplicate URL parsing in logging middleware
- Batch capability DELETE + INSERTs for atomicity (DB.batch)
- Remove unused JSX config from tsconfig (pure JSON API)
- Normalize LogsBinding to interface for consistency

commit.message:
fix: prevent black images from HEIC files and canvas decode failures

- Skip canvas compression for HEIC/HEIF files (iOS default format) —
  canvas drawImage silently produces black output for HEIC
- Add blob size sanity check (>2KB) to detect empty canvas output
  and fall back to original file instead of uploading a black image
- Replace reject() with resolve(file) on img/reader errors so photos
  are never silently lost on decode failure
- Fix trophy detection: parseInt → parseFloat for length comparison
  against DB NUMERIC values (e.g. 42 >= 42.5 was incorrectly false)
- Fix threshold/max_length null check: truthy → != null

commit.message:
Stop silently swallowing apiswitch failures in _aiswitch_apply

apiswitch was called with 2>/dev/null which hid the "No LogiQ token"
warning and let the apply continue — resulting in the desktop apps
being restarted with an empty ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN. Now redirect
apiswitch output to /dev/tty (so the user sees errors) and return
early on failure, preventing a broken profile from being applied.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix live data race: wait for auth before fetching entity data

The page component fired loadLiveData() on both onMount and $effect,
racing with the root layout's auth.refresh(). Both requests hit the API
with no token, got 401, and silently fell back to mock data.

Fix: remove redundant onMount, gate $effect on !$authLoading so data
fetch waits until auth initialization completes.

commit.message:
Deploy multiple PFNs via helm releases (#19009)

* [forge] Remove old direct-install PFN code from K8s backend

Delete the fullnode.rs module that created PFNs as individual K8s
resources (StatefulSet, ConfigMap, PVC, Service). This path is being
replaced by the forge PFN deployer which uses helm releases.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [forge] Replace num_pfns with pfn-deployments in PFN deployer config

Replace the num_pfns config key with pfn-deployments, an array of
PFN deployment entries each specifying a helmReleaseName. This enables
the deployer to create multiple helm releases (one per PFN) instead of
a single release that only creates one PFN.

with_num_pfns(N) now generates N entries like [{helmReleaseName: "pfn-0"},
{helmReleaseName: "pfn-1"}, ...] in pfn-deployments, with pfn-values
serving as the shared base values for all deployments.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [forge] Add PFN node config support and bump deployer version

Add with_pfn_override_node_config_fn() to ForgeConfig for specifying
PFN node configs that get injected into pfn-values.fullnode.config.
Bump forge deployer image tag to 82fd1a3d48c698ff9ecef8ab4f9c5141fc94d22a.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(evolve): fix broken claude invocations and grep syntax errors

The --max-budget-usd removal left blank lines in shell continuations,
causing --output-format to be interpreted as a command (exit code 127).
Fixed all three claude invocations (plan, impl, reflect).

Also fixed grep -c piped through || echo 0 producing multiline output
that broke [[ arithmetic comparisons. Now uses || true with default
variable assignment.

commit.message:
Fix empty ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN in launchd env

_aiswitch_launchd_env was calling _aiswitch_logiq_key() to get the
token, but the token is only in shell env (OPENAI_API_KEY from
~/.apienv), not in Keychain. Since _aiswitch_apply sources ~/.apienv
before calling _aiswitch_launchd_env, ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN is already
set in the current shell at that point — use it directly, with
keychain as fallback.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Reorganize repo structure for clarity

- preprocessing/: DATA.py renamed to preprocess.py, notebook moved here
- training/: added TRAIN_melanoma.ipynb
- inference/: consolidated live_inter/ scripts + added predict.py

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use site.utilityRate.rateStructure instead of utilityAccounts[0].rateData in savings-calculator

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(deploy): exclude WAL files from rsync --delete + checkpoint before restart

Root cause of recurring data loss: rsync --delete was removing
*.db-wal and *.db-shm files during deployment because only *.db
was excluded. With SQLite WAL mode enabled, uncommitted writes
in the WAL file were lost on every deploy.

Fixes:
- Add --exclude='*.db-wal' --exclude='*.db-shm' --exclude='*.db-journal'
- Add PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE) before rsync and before restart
  to flush all pending writes to the main DB file

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
